clauses.statement: Source diffs limited to src/usage-report.js must not activate agent_workflow solely because Story or Spec text mentions agent, subagent, review, gate, artifact, Codex, or Claude telemetry.
clauses.rationale: Prevents read-only reporting changes from being forced through workflow-heavy gates.

story_refs.text_snippet: A change limited to src/usage-report.js plus docs/tests is classified as read-only reporting, not agent_workflow.

test_refs.case: usage-report metrics changes stay reporting-scoped instead of workflow-gated

clauses.statement: Common Judgment Spine must use surface reporting for read-only usage-report metric changes and require focused test plus runtime-path evidence.
clauses.rationale: Reporting metrics are runtime-output precision changes, not workflow orchestration changes.

story_refs.text_snippet: Common Judgment Spine uses a reporting surface for usage-report metrics and accepts focused test plus runtime-path evidence.

test_refs.case: usage-report metrics changes stay reporting-scoped instead of workflow-gated

clauses.statement: Scheduler blueprint detection must not treat lifecycle metric wording such as interval as scheduled-job infrastructure by itself.
clauses.rationale: Telemetry language should not trigger scheduler infrastructure gates unless actual scheduling language or paths exist.

story_refs.text_snippet: The architecture blueprint scheduler gate is not created from lifecycle interval wording alone.

test_refs.case: usage-report metrics changes stay reporting-scoped instead of workflow-gated

clauses.statement: Story text containing ADR-unnecessary must satisfy Architecture Gate as an explicit no-ADR decision.
clauses.rationale: The Story is the authoring surface used by operators for this no-new-architecture decision.

story_refs.text_snippet: Story-level ADR-unnecessary is honored by Architecture Gate.

test_refs.case: usage-report metrics changes stay reporting-scoped instead of workflow-gated

clauses.statement: Given a real agent workflow source path, when pr prepare runs, then the existing workflow evidence requirements still apply.
clauses.rationale: The exception must not weaken Senior Engineering Judgment for actual orchestration changes.

story_refs.text_snippet: Real agent workflow changes still require workflow replay evidence and keep existing agent_workflow route behavior.

test_refs.case: pr prepare emits Engineering Judgment route, route-specific gates, and DAG connectivity

diagrams.mermaid:
flowchart TD
  Diff["PR diff"] --> Classify["PR Gate classification"]
  Classify --> UsageReport{"Only src/usage-report.js source?"}
  UsageReport -->|yes| Reporting["reporting surface"]
  UsageReport -->|no| Workflow["normal workflow/source surface"]
  Reporting --> Evidence["focused test plus runtime path evidence"]
  Workflow --> WorkflowEvidence["workflow replay evidence remains required when applicable"]
  Evidence --> GateDag["Gate DAG"]
  WorkflowEvidence --> GateDag
diagrams.rationale: Flow diagram for the reporting exception and the preserved workflow-heavy path.
